_doc: Hotel booking config for one edition. Captures the official hotel, the rates, the coverage rules (who ELDK pays for and how many nights), and the EXCEPTIONS - per-speaker-type rules and per-person overrides. The app's hotel-cost engine reads only this file; it has no hardcoded rates or rules.

officialHotel.name: Bella Sky Copenhagen
officialHotel._doc: Single official hotel. groupCode is the hotel's group-booking reference, useful on confirmations.

rates._doc: singleRoomStay / doubleRoomStay = the standard package price for the WHOLE standard stay, per room, covering both occupants of a double. extraNightSingle / extraNightDouble = price PER NIGHT beyond the covered allowance. extraNight* values 598/698 are PLACEHOLDERS - replace with the hotel's real per-night rate before go-live.

coverageRules._doc: How many nights ELDK pays, by role / speaker type. nightsCovered: an integer, or "all" for every night of the booking. The cost engine: covered nights cost the crew member 0; nights beyond the allowance are billed at the extraNight rate. Standard checkout is the main day, so a standard booking owes 0.

masterclass._doc: Master class speaker: ELDK pays 2 nights (the master class is the pre-day, so they stay an extra night).

coverageRules._resolution: Resolve coverage in this order: (1) a per-person override in personOverrides below; (2) for speakers, bySpeakerType using the speaker's type; (3) byRole using the person's role; (4) default.

personOverrides.reason: Keynote - ELDK covers the full stay by agreement
personOverrides._doc: EXCEPTIONS for named individuals. This is the mechanism for 'for some speakers we pay for all nights'. Add one entry per person who differs from the standard rule. Matched by email (the login key). reason is shown to organizers, not the crew member. Remove the example entry; it is illustrative only.

speakerTypes._doc: Speaker types drive coverage via coverageRules.bySpeakerType. A speaker's type is set on their profile.

confirmationNumbers._doc: Hotel confirmation numbers are entered by organizers (crew see them read-only), and a calendar invite (.ics) is sent to the crew member. Numbers can be bulk-imported from the hotel's group confirmation list (see the Confirmed Hotel Information export), keyed on email.

roomNightForecast._doc: The hotel needs an EARLY, and then revised, count of how many ROOMS to block PER NIGHT. They do not care who stays - only the nightly room count. The hub computes this from all hotel bookings: for each night in the stay window, count the rooms occupied that night. IMPORTANT: a double room shared with a partner is still ONE room - the forecast counts rooms, not guests. Output is a per-date table (date -> rooms needed) the organizer can export and send to the hotel; it refreshes as bookings change.

stayWindow._doc: The full range of possible hotel nights for this edition - the forecast has one column/row per date in this window. DERIVED from the event dates (pre-day minus arrival buffer .. day2 plus departure buffer); set explicitly here so it is unambiguous. CONFIRM against the real ELDK27 arrival/departure pattern - the ELDK26 roster spanned 21-28 Feb because that event was late February; ELDK27 is 8-10 Feb so this window differs.

export._doc: Organizer can export the forecast (date, rooms-needed) as a file to hand the hotel.
